rulings.Case Title: CACV OF COLORADO, LLC VS. WAYNE WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice To Junior Lien Holders Of Hearing On Appliation For Order And Judgment For Sale Of Dwelling
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 14, 2012, Line 1. Plaintiff'S Notice To Junior Lien Holders Of Hearing On Appliation For Order And Judgment For Sale Of Dwelling. Denied. Mr. Brkich's declaration provides an insufficient basis for his opinion of the fair market value. Even if his opinion that the market value is $398,900 is accepted, it is not clear that the total of Defendant's homestead exemption and the known liens (excluding the one recorded by Plaintiff/Assignee) is more than the fair market value of Defendant's one-third interest in the Property. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CACV OF COLORADO, LLC VS. WAYNE WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 14, 2012, Line 1. ASSIGNEE GRYPHON SOLUTIONS, LLC.'S Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Granted. Section 703.050(c) governs. Because the property was levied on February 16, 2012, the version of section 704.730 in effect at that time controls. The version of section 704.730 in effect on February 16, 2012 provides for a $75,000 homestead exemption for Defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK ALIOTO et al VS. WILLIAM F. GARLOCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel And For Evisdentiary, Termination And Monetary Sanctions Against Pltfs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on mONday, May 14, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT CHRIS PASTENA, MICHAEL PASTENA, JESSE BRANSTETTER, CULINARY ARTS STUDIOS LLC'S Motion To Compel And For Evidentiary, Termination And Monetary Sanctions Against PlAINTIFFS. Pro Tem Judge James P. Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. The motion is unopposed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: James P. Watson

rulings.Case Title: CENTURY 21 PINNACLE AKA PINNACLE HOMES & ESTATES VS. JOSE L. CAMPOS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion/Pettiiton To Compel Arb And Request To Stay Procedings Until Completion Of Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 14, 2012, Line 4. PLAINTIFF CENTURY 21 PINNACLE AKA PINNACLE HOMES & ESTATES' Notice Of Hearing On Motion/Petition To Compel Arbitration And Request To Stay Procedings Until Completion Of Arbitration. The court reinstates its tentative ruling from April 19 as follows due to defendants'' acknowledgment that their argument based on Code of Civil Procedure 1281.5(b) lacked merit: Granted. The filing of this action was not a waiver or forfeiture of the right to arbitrate. Paragraph 19B(2)(iv) permits the filing of an action to record a lis pendens. The failure to request mediation earlier is not a waiver or forfeiture of the right to arbitrate. Paragraph 19A provides that the waiver of attorney's fees is the penalty for failing to pursue mediation. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: IN THE MATTER OF THE PETITION OF MANDEVILLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Petition For Approval Of Transfer Of Structured Settlewment Payment Rights
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 14, 2012, Line 6. PETITIONER IN THE MATTER OF THE PETITION OF MANDEVILLA'S Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Approval Of Transfer Of Structured Settlement Payment Rights. Denied. Mr. Pipkins is barred from transferring any of the structured settlement payments related to annuity contracts issued by John Hancock Life Insurance Company, including annuity contract 3221324, to third parties until the judgment in the federal case titled John Hancock Life Insurance Co. v. Donald C. Pipkins, action no. 3-11-CV-04992-CRB, is satisfied. Future structured settlement payments related to annuity contracts issued by John Hancock to which Mr. Pipkins is entitled may be applied to the judgment until it is satisfied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
